options.A: You can use the _ to ensure that all sentences have the same length after tokenization, matching the length of
options.B: the longest sentence among the input sentences

corrections.question_text: Your team member wants to set up a PyTorch training loop but places the model in evaluation mode before beginning training epochs. What corrections should be made to structure the training loop properly?

options.A: Skip setting model mode during training
options.B: Use the model.eval() before training starts
options.C: Replace the optimizer with a loss function
options.D: Use the model.train() at the start of each training epoch

corrections.correct_option_text: Use the model.train() at the start of each training epoch

corrections.question_text: In an encoder-decoder RNN architecture, how is the output sequence typically generated during translation? The model merges input and output vectors

options.A: The encoder predicts the complete output
options.B: The decoder repeats the same tokens
options.C: The decoder modules with RNN cells

corrections.correct_option_text: The encoder predicts the complete output

corrections.question_text:
Suppose an algorithm runs in time (200 log_2{2} (n) + 250) time in the worst case as a function of the input size n, which of the options below are correct?

The running time is Θ(( log_{10}(n) )) since ( log_{10}(n) = f(log_2(n)/ log_2(10))) is a constant factor × log(_2{(n)}.)

It is impossible for an algorithm to have a running time that is smaller than the input size.

The running time of the algorithm is O(( n )).

options.A: The running time is Θ(( log_{10}(n) )) since ( log_{10}(n) = f(log_2(n)/ log_2(10))) is a constant factor × log(_2{(n)}.)
options.B: It is impossible for an algorithm to have a running time that is smaller than the input size.
options.C: The running time of the algorithm is O(( n )).

corrections.question_text: Consider a minheap that is presented as a tree below:

options.A: The minheap property is that each element is smaller than or equal to its children (if they exist).
options.B: If an element 2 is inserted into the heap at the end of the array, the min heap property will continue to be true.
options.C: The heap when written as an array will be [-5, 8, 4, 12, 10, 6, 19, 13, 17, 11, 12].
options.D: The second smallest element of a minheap is always the second element of the array.
options.E: The minheap property is that each element is smaller than its left child and larger than its right child.

corrections.correct_option_text: The second smallest element of a minheap is always the second element of the array.
